scenario: A company uses Power Automate cloud flows. Flows run in the context of a user with the System Administrator security role. An action in the flow returns confidential data from a Microsoft Dataverse table. You need to prevent this confidential data from being visible in the flow run history logs.
q: For each statement, select Yes if it is true, otherwise No.

rows.s: Marking the output variable as sensitive hides the data in the logs.
Adding a masking rule to the columns in the Dataverse table hides the data in the logs.
Turning on the Secure Outputs property on the action hides the data in the logs.

why: Marking a variable as sensitive is a DESKTOP-flow feature, not a cloud-flow feature, so it does not hide data in a cloud flow's run history (statement 1 = No). Column masking rules apply a regex to obfuscate data only for UNAUTHORIZED users; because the flow runs as a System Administrator it reads the unmasked value and stores it in the logs (statement 2 = No). Turning on the Secure Outputs (Secure Inputs/Outputs) property on the action prevents the action's output from being stored/shown in the flow run history, hiding the confidential data (statement 3 = Yes).
